zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert I de FIENNES de HEUCHIN於1248年出生在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德維其雷斯時，他的父親Enguerrand de Fiennes56歲，母親Agnès de Condé de Morialmes33歲。他和Claude De Luxembourg至少有6個兒子。他去世在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯。

nameMeanings.name: HEUCHIN
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Essex): of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Huchon, a double diminutive of Hue (see Hugh ) common in Picardy, corresponding to Old French Hueçon, Huesson, modern French Husson. This was used as a personal name in Scotland, where it also became a surname and was adopted in Gaelic as Huisdean or Uisdean; see McCutcheon and compare Hutch .

variants.name: Hutchinson
meanings: English (northern): patronymic from the Old French personal name Hucheon (see Houchin ) + -son.
Irish: in Ireland and Scotland, adopted for Mac Uisdein and Mac Uistin; see McCutcheon .

History: Anne Marbury Hutchinson (1591–1643) and her husband William came from Lincolnshire, England, to MA in 1634. A religious dissident, she led the first attack on the Puritans and was banished from the Massachusetts Bay Colony. She moved to RI in 1638, and, after her husband's death in 1642, settled in NY, where the Hutchinson River was named in her honor. Ironically, the Hutchinson name stayed on in MA and one of her descendants, Thomas Hutchinson (1711–80), was royal governor of Massachusetts Bay Colony.

variants.name: Houchen
meanings: English (Norfolk): of Norman origin, a variant of   Houchin  .

variants.name: Houchins
meanings: English: patronymic from   Houchin  .

variants.name: Hutcheon
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Angus): variant of   Houchin  .

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert I de FIENNES de HEUCHIN was born in 1248, in Wicres, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, his father, Enguerrand de Fiennes, was 56 and his mother, Agnès de Condé de Morialmes, was 33. He had at least 6 sons with Claude De Luxembourg. He died in Heuchin, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert I de FIENNES de HEUCHIN est né en 1248, à Wicres, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Enguerrand de Fiennes, avait 56 ans et sa mère, Agnès de Condé de Morialmes, avait 33 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils avec Claude De Luxembourg. Il est décédé à Heuchin, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert I de FIENNES de HEUCHIN 1248 in Wicres, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Enguerrand de Fiennes 56 und seine Mutter Agnès de Condé de Morialmes 33. Er hatte mit Claude De Luxembourg mindestens 6 Söhne. Er starb in Heuchin, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert I de FIENNES de HEUCHIN è nato il nel 1248 nel Wicres, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, il suo padre, Enguerrand de Fiennes, aveva 56 anni e il suo madre, Agnès de Condé de Morialmes, aveva 33 anni. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli con Claude De Luxembourg. Morì nel Heuchin, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert I de FIENNES de HEUCHINが1248年にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードウィクレスで生まれた時，父Enguerrand de Fiennesは56歳，母Agnès de Condé de Morialmesは33歳であった。彼はClaude De Luxembourgとの間に少なくとも6人の息子をもうけた。彼はフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスプアス-デ-カレイスヘウチンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert I de FIENNES de HEUCHIN(이)가 1248년 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 위크레스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Enguerrand de Fiennes(은)는 56세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnès de Condé de Morialmes(은)는 33세였다. 그는 Claude De Luxembourg(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 파스-데-캘레이 헤우친에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert I de FIENNES de HEUCHIN родился в 1248 г. в местности викресь, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция, его отцу по имени Enguerrand de Fiennes было 56 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnès de Condé de Morialmes, 33. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей от Claude De Luxembourg. Он умер в местности хеучин, пас-де-калеи, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция.
